# SkyTemple Files

{{ build }} {{ pypi-version }} {{ pypi-downloads }} {{ pypi-license }} {{ pypi-pyversions }} {{ discord }}

Python library to work with the files inside the ROM of Pokémon Mystery Dungeon Explorers of Sky.

The main handlers for file types can be found in the members of the class `skytemple_files.common.types.file_types.FileType`.

You can find the handlers inside the packages of this Python package, with `README.rst` files explaining the
file formats. Additional documentation for misc. things can be found in the `/docs` directory. For usage examples
see the `dbg` packages and the SkyTemple main application.

Directly in the package (`skytemple_files` directory) you can find a few an example script that help with understanding
how to use this library. After installing the package you will have is as a cli command: `skytemple_export_maps`.

In addition to the dependencies in the `requirements.txt` and `pyproject.toml`, ARMIPS must
be installed and in the system's `PATH` to be able to apply ROM patches.

## Extras

### spritecollab

With the `spritecollab` extra, the package `skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client` is available to
interact with the SpriteCollab GraphQL server for retrieving portrait and sprite assets by the community.

More information:

- <https://sprites.pmdcollab.org>
- <https://spriteserver.pmdcollab.org>
- <https://github.com/PMDCollab/SpriteCollab>
- <https://github.com/PMDCollab/spritecollab-srv>

modules.name: skytemple_files.export_maps
documentation:
This is a sample Python CLI script that uses SkyTemple Files to export all maps of the game,
including sprites of objects and actors on them.

This is also an example on how to use the following file handlers:
- BMA, BPL, PPC, BPA
- BG_LIST_DAT
- DUNGEON_BIN
- DMA, DPC, DPCI, DPL, DPLA
- MAPPA
- MD
- SSA
- LSD
- WAN, WAT
- (BIN_PACK)
- (COMMON_AT)
- (Compressions)
- (Hardcoded Lists)
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: skytemple_files.export_maps.draw_actor
documentation: Draws the sprite for an actor

functions.name: skytemple_files.export_maps.draw_object
documentation: Draws the sprite for an object

modules.name: skytemple_files.data.monster_xml
documentation:
XML export / import for MD, WAZA_P and LEVEL_BIN_ENTRY.
Theoretically compatible with ppmdu, but contains some new field names for some things.

functions.name: skytemple_files.data.monster_xml.monster_xml_export
documentation:
Exports properties of all given things as an XML file. If a second Md entry is given,
the first must also be given.
Names are a dict wher each key is the language and values are (Name, Category).

functions.name: skytemple_files.data.monster_xml.monster_xml_import
documentation:
Imports the available data from the XML into the models and lists given.
The lists can already be filled, they will be cleared and re-filled when data is avaiable.
Returns the game version.

modules.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.ssb_number
documentation:
Handling SSB encoded numbers. From pmdsky-debug's documentation for `ScriptParamToInt`:

>   Converts the given opcode parameter to a signed integer.
>
>   The parameter will be returned unchanged unless one of its two most significant bits (0x8000 and 0x4000) are set,
>   in which case both bits will be cleared and the original value will be modified according to the following two
>   rules:
>   - If the 0x4000 bit is set (sign bit), the value will be set to -16384 + value.
>   - If the 0x8000 bit is set (fixed-point flag), the value will be set to value / 256, rounded down.
>   Both rules can be applied, in the same order as listed, if both conditions are met.
>
>   r0: Parameter to convert
>   return: The input parameter, as a signed integer

functions.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.ssb_number.parse_ssb_encoding
documentation: Convert a ssb encoded number into either a raw 15-bit signed integer or a fixed point parameter

classes.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.script_compiler.ScriptCompiler

methods.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.script_compiler.ScriptCompiler.compile_ssbscript
documentation:
Compile SSBScript into a SSB model

:raises: ParseError: On parsing errors
:raises: SsbCompilerError: On logical compiling errors (eg. unknown opcodes)
:raises: ValueError: On misc. logical compiling errors (eg. unknown constants)

methods.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.script_compiler.ScriptCompiler.compile_explorerscript
documentation:
Compile ExplorerScript into a SSB model. Returns the Ssb model, the source map, and a list of macros
that were used in the ExplorerScript file.

lookup_paths is the list of include lookup paths.

:raises: ParseError: On parsing errors
:raises: SsbCompilerError: On logical compiling errors (eg. unknown opcodes)
:raises: ValueError: On misc. logical compiling errors (eg. unknown constants)

methods.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.constants.SsbConstant.collect_all
documentation: Collects all possible constants from the given ROM data

Class to map entries from the PPMDU script config (and other sources)
to constants for use in ssb representations.

Pmd2Script* objects can be converted into this by using create_for.

The representation as a string can be retrieved using str(self) or repr(self).

The constructor can be used with any string that this class represents,
(and an instance of Pmd2ScriptData) and be converted back with convert_to_script_entity.
Giving it an unknown constant_as_string value will raise a ValueError.

documentation: Assert that self and other are equal. If they are not, the raised AssertionError will contain details.

methods.name: skytemple_files.script.ssb.flow.SsbFlow.bfs_generator
documentation:
Basically the same as graph.bfsiter with advanced (but as a generator).
However the order of vertices honors the flow_level of it's in edge. It also takes special Ssb flow
rules into account.

Represents the flow of all Ssb routines as a graph. It can be compared with other graphs,
to check if two Ssb models are logically the same.
This uses the same base mechanisms / graphing logic as the ExplorerScript compiler.

modules.name: skytemple_files.common.impl_cfg
documentation:
Implementation configuration.
Using this module, you can switch between implementations (Python or native using Rust) for
all modules that support native code.

You should only modify this before any other skytemple_files component is loaded,
otherwise the behaviour could be unexpected.

This defaults to use the Python implementations if SKYTEMPLE_USE_NATIVE is not set.
If it is set, it defaults to the native implementations.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWValue

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWValue.from_c_type
documentation:
Used to create an instance of one of the subclasses of this class depending on the specified C type.
Pmdsky-debug custom types are also supported.
\:param type_str: String that represents the C type to use as a reference to create the instance. Must be one
of the types supported by the subclasses of this class.
\:param offset: Offset within the binary to read/write to.
\:return: The new instance of one of the subclasses of this class
\:raises UnsupportedTypeError: If the specified C type is not supported

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWValue.read_str
documentation:
Reads the value from the binary and offset specified when instantiating the class and converts it to a string.
\:param binary: Binary the data should be read from
\:param index: If > 0, the operation will be treated as an array element access, and the array element on the
given index will be returned.
\:return: The value represented by this instance, as a string.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWValue.write_str
documentation:
Writes the given value to the binary and offset specified when instantiating the class. This method will
attempt to convert the given string to the type represented by this instance.
\:param binary: Binary the data should be written to
\:param value: Value to write
\:param index: If > 0, the operation will be treated as an array element access, and the array element on the
given index will be overwritten.
\:raises ValueError: If the given string cannot be converted to the underlying data type.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWValue._type_size
documentation: Returns the size of the type represented by this instance

The RWValue class, short for "Readable/Writable Value", allows reading or writing a single value to a given offset
in one of the binaries.

This abstract class allows reading an writing values as strings. Each subclass allows reading and writing them as
concrete data types.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWFx6416Value
documentation:
RWValue subclass for values of type Fx64_16, which defines 64-bit signed fixed-point numbers with 16 fraction bits.
It represents the number (upper << 16) + (lower >> 16) + (lower & 0xFFFF) * 2^-16.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWFx3216Value
documentation:
RWValue subclass for values of type Fx32_16, which defines 32-bit signed fixed-point numbers with 16 fraction bits.
It represents the number (value >> 16) + (value & 0xFFFF) * 2^-16.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWFx328Value
documentation:
RWValue subclass for values of type Fx32_8, which defines 32-bit signed fixed-point numbers with 8 fraction bits.
It represents the number (value >> 24) + (value & 0xFF) * 2^-8.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWShiftedImmediateValue

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWShiftedImmediateValue._get_shifted_immediate
documentation:
Given an integer, attempts to encode it as a shifted immediate
\:param value: Original value to encode
\:return: Tuple containing the base B and rotation parameter R
\:raises ValueError: If the specified value cannot be expressed as a shifted immediate

RWValue subclass for shifted immediate values that are embedded into ARM instructions. The values are represented
by a base B (0-255) and a rotation parameter R (0-15). Values are encoded as B >>> (R * 2), where ">>>" is the
rotate right operator.
This class does not support array indexing.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.rw_value.RWCharArrayValue
documentation: RWValue subclass for char arrays. Does not support array indexing.

documentation: Utility functions for dealing with sprites

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.sprite_util.check_and_correct_monster_sprite_size
documentation:
Check that the data in the Pokémon sprite-size metadata
table matches the currently selected sprite of the Pokémon.
If not, change the value and save it.

Arguments:
    - md_target: The MD entry to modify. This should be one of `md_gender1` or `md_gender2`.
    - md_gender1: The primary gender entry.
    - md_gender2: The secondary gender entry or None.
    - monster_bin: monster.bin sprite file
    - sprite_size_table: Sprite size metadata table
    - is_expand_poke_list_patch_applied: Whether ExpandPokeList is applied.

Returns a list of changes if a change was performed. Each element contains a tuple:
- Change description (localized)
- Old value
- New value

If changes were performed the data in the following input parameters may be modified and should
be written back to ROM:

- md_target
- sprite_size_table

documentation:
Works with a list of lists of rgb color palettes and returns a modified copy.

Returns a list that does not contain duplicate colors. This is done by slightly changing
the color values of duplicate colors.

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.util.get_ppmdu_config_for_rom
documentation:
Returns the Pmd2Data for the given ROM.
If the ROM is not a valid and supported PMD EoS ROM, raises ValueError.

The configuration is loaded from the pmd2data.xml using the XML logic described in the README.rst
of the `skytemple_files.common.ppmdu_config` package.

Additionally supported data from the ROM is loaded and replaces the data loaded from the XML, if possible.
See the README.rst for the package `skytemple_files.common.ppmdu_config.rom_data` for more information.

documentation: Yield successive n-sized chunks from lst.

documentation:
Inserts an element value at index index in lst. If the list is not big enough,
it is enlarged and empty slots are filled with the return value of filler_fn.

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.util.simple_quant
documentation:
Simple single-palette image quantization. Reduces to 15 colors and adds one transparent color at index 0.
The transparent (alpha=0) pixels in the input image are converted to that color (if can_have_transparency=True).
If you need to do tiled multi-palette quantization, use Tilequant instead!

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.util.mutate_sequence
documentation:
This context manager provides the attribute sequence value behind the attribute as a list (copy),
and then assigns the attribute to that list. So while you can "mutate" the "original" sequence this way,
it's slow. If performance matters at all consider doing it differently.
TODO: Better typing (probably impossible?)

modules.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image
documentation: Common module for reading and writing tiled indexed rgb 4bpp images

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.to_pil
documentation:
Convert all tiles referenced in tile_mapping to one big PIL image.

The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.
If ignore_flip_bits is set, tiles are not flipped.

tiling_width/height control how many tiles form a chunk.

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.to_pil_tiled
documentation:
Convert all tiles of the image into separate PIL images.
Each image has one palette with 16 colors.
If ignore_flip_bits is set, tiles are not flipped.

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.from_pil
documentation:
Modify the image data in the tiled image by importing the passed PIL.

The passed tiled image will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index
is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).
The index of the tile stored is determined by the image dimensions and the tiling width and height.

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

If optimize is True, we check each read tile, if this tile or a flipped version already exists in the data set,
if so we re-use the tile.

palette_offset can be used to offset all color values in the original PIL image by the number of x palettes,
in the case where palettes were merged.

Returns (tiles, tile mappings, palettes)

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.search_for_chunk
documentation:
In the provided list of tile mappings, find an existing chunk.
Returns the position of the first tile of the chunk or None if not found.
The chunk dimensions are derived from the passed chunk.

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.search_for_tile_with_sum
documentation:
Search for the tile, or a flipped version of it, in tiles and return the index and flipped state
Increases performance by comparing the bytes sum of each tile before actually compare them

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.search_for_tile
documentation: Search for the tile, or a flipped version of it, in tiles and return the index and flipped state

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image._flip_tile_x
documentation: Flip all pixels in tile on the x-axis

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image._flip_tile_y
documentation: Flip all pixels in tile on the y-axis

functions.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image._px_pos_flipped
documentation:
Returns the flipped x and y position for a pixel in a fixed size image.
If x and/or y actually get flipped is controled by the flip_ params.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.TilemapEntry.to_int
documentation: Converts tile map entry back into the byte format used by game

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.tiled_image.TilemapEntry.from_int
documentation: Create a tile map entry from the common two byte format used by the game

modules.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client
documentation:
This module requires the `spritecollab` extra.

Utilities to interact with an instance of a spritecollab-rs GraphQL
server to retrieve metadata, portraits and sprites & tools to import
them into a ROM.

For details about the schema and queries, please read the schema
documentation at the server.

Use `SpriteCollabClient` to get started.

class_variables.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.MonsterFormInfo.form_path
documentation: Does not include the monster ID.

class_variables.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.MonsterFormInfo.full_form_name
documentation: This includes the monster name.

class_variables.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.MonsterFormInfo.portraits_modified_date
documentation: This is None on errors.

class_variables.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.MonsterFormInfo.sprites_modified_date
documentation: This is None on errors.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.fetch_config
documentation: Query the server SpriteCollab configuration.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.list_monster_forms
documentation:
Fetches some basic metadata about all monster forms. Each entry in the returned list is one
form, the list is sorted by index.
If `with_preview_portrait` the "Normal" emotion portrait is also returned.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.monster_form_details
documentation:
Returns details about monster forms for a monster, including credits and all portraits and sprites ready to be
fetched. `monsters_and_forms` is a list of tuples, where the first entry is the
monster ID and the second the form path.

The returned list contains details for monsters in the same order as `monsters_and_forms`.

Raises an error if any path could not be resolved to a valid form.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.fetch_portraits
documentation:
Fetch portraits for the given forms. `monsters_and_forms` is a list of tuples, where the first entry is the
monster ID and the second the form path.

The portraits are converted into a list of KaoImages, where None entries are slots that are either not available
or were blacklisted or not whitelisted. The returned list contains portraits for monsters in the same order
as `monsters_and_forms`.

Raises an error if any path could not be resolved to a valid form.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.fetch_sprites
documentation:
Fetch sprites for the given forms. `monsters_and_forms` is a list of tuples, where the first entry is the
monster ID and the second the form path.

The sprites are converted into a merged WanFile. The returned list contains sprites for monsters in the same
order as `monsters_and_forms`. The tuples in the list contain the wan file, a single Pmd2Sprite with the action
mappings, and the shadow size.

`actions` must be a list with one entry for each entry in `monsters_and_forms`. Each entry is a list of
actions that should be imported (if they don't exist on SpriteCollab, they are ignored). Alternatively
an entry can be None, in which case all available actions will be imported.

If `copy_to_event_sleep_if_missing` is True and `EventSleep` and/or `Laying` and/or `Waking` are requested for
a form but not available, all of those that were requested are set as a copy of `Sleep` instead.
This only happens if the `Sleep` action is requested for a form.

Raises an error if any path could not be resolved to a valid form.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabSession.execute_query
documentation: Note that you should upcast the returned type to match your actual query subtype.

An active session on a SpriteCollab server.

Don't construct this manually, use `async with` on an instance of `SpriteCollabClient`.
Do not use instances of this outside said `async with` blocks.

classes.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabClient

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabClient.__init__
documentation:
Create a new client instance.
The last `cache_size` requests are cached (including fetched assets).
To disable the cache, set `cache_size` to 0.
If `use_ssl` is set to `False`, requests are always made via HTTP (SSL is disabled). "<https://>" at the start of
the provided `server_url` is replaced with "<http://>" then. HTTP traffic may still be redirected to SSL.
If `use_certifi_ssl` is used, then the certificate of the `certifi` package are used.
The caller must make sure that package is installed.

If you specify a custom request adapter, the `cache_size`, `use_ssl` and `use_certifi_ssl` parameters
are ignored.

methods.name: skytemple_files.common.spritecollab.client.SpriteCollabClient.flush_cache
documentation: Clears the cache. This is also always done when creating a new session with `async with`.

Client to connect to a SpriteCollab server. Use `async with` to get
a tuple containing a session (`SpriteCollabSession`) and the DSL for the schema.

1 means there is a wall / water. Out of bounds is read as floor, unless treat_outside_as_wall,
then it's water/wall.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dma._model.Dma.get
documentation:
Returns all three variations (chunk ids) set for this dungeon tile configuration.
neighbors_same is a bitfield with the bits for the directions set to 1 if the neighbor at this
position has the same type as the tile at this position.
TIP: For neighbors_same, use the bit flags in DmaNeighbor.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dma._model.Dma.get_extra
documentation:
Returns a few extra chunk variations for the given type.
How they are used exactly by the game is currently not know,
this interface could change if we find out.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dma._model.Dma.set
documentation: Sets the mapping for the given configuration and the given variation of it.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dma._model.Dma.set_extra
documentation: Sets and extra tile entry.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dma._model.Dma.to_pil
documentation:
For debugging only, the output image contains some labels, etc. Use get(...) instead to
get a chunk mapping and then render that chunk by extracting it from the image returned by
bpc.chunks_to_pil(...)

Converts the chunks of the DMA into an image. Works like bpc.chunks_to_pil,
but uses the chunk mappings stored in this file to place them instead.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.to_pil_single_layer
documentation:
Converts one layer of the map into an image. The exported image has the same format as expected by from_pil.
Exported is a single frame.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

0: lower layer
1: upper layer

Example, of how to export and then import again using images:
    >>> l_upper = bma.to_pil_single_layer(bpc, bpl.palettes, bpas, 1)
    >>> l_lower = bma.to_pil_single_layer(bpc, bpl.palettes, bpas, 0)
    >>> bma.from_pil(bpc, bpl, l_lower, l_upper)

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.to_pil
documentation:
Converts the entire map into an image, as shown in the game. Each PIL image in the list returned is one
frame. The palettes argument can be retrieved from the map's BPL (bpl.palettes).

The method does not care about frame speeds. Each step of animation is simply returned as a new image,
so if BPAs use different frame speeds, this is ignored; they effectively run at the same speed.
If BPAs are using a different amount of frames per tile, the length of returned list of images will be the lowest
common multiple of the different frame lengths.

If pal_ani=True, then also includes palette animations.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.from_pil
documentation:
Import an entire map from one or two images (for each layer).
Changes all tiles, tilemappings and chunks in the BPC and re-writes the two layer mappings of the BMA.
Imports the palettes of the image to the BPL.
The palettes of the images passed into this method must either identical or can be merged.
The how_many_palettes_lower_layer parameter controls how many palettes
from the lower layer image will then be used.

The passed PIL will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index in the tile mapping for this
coordinate is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing up to 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.
The force_import option may be ignored by implementations, in that case the color is always just
replaced with 0.

Does not import animations. BPA tiles must be manually mapped to the tilemappings of the BPC after the import.
BPL palette animations are not modified.

The input images must have the same dimensions as the BMA (same dimensions as to_pil_single_layer would export).
The input image can have a different number of layers, than the BMA. BPC and BMA layers are changed accordingly.

BMA collision and data layer are not modified.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.remove_upper_layer
documentation: Remove the upper layer. Silently does nothing when it doesn't exist.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.add_upper_layer
documentation: Add an upper layer. Silently does nothing when it already exists.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.resize
documentation:
Change the dimensions of the map. Existing tiles and chunks will keep their position in the grid.
If the size is reduced, all tiles and chunks that are moved out of the new dimension box are removed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma.protocol.BmaProtocol.place_chunk

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.to_pil_single_layer
documentation:
Converts one layer of the map into an image. The exported image has the same format as expected by from_pil.
Exported is a single frame.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

0: lower layer
1: upper layer

Example, of how to export and then import again using images:
    >>> l_upper = bma.to_pil_single_layer(bpc, bpl.palettes, bpas, 1)

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.to_pil
documentation:
Converts the entire map into an image, as shown in the game. Each PIL image in the list returned is one
frame. The palettes argument can be retrieved from the map's BPL (bpl.palettes).

The method does not care about frame speeds. Each step of animation is simply returned as a new image,
so if BPAs use different frame speeds, this is ignored; they effectively run at the same speed.
If BPAs are using a different amount of frames per tile, the length of returned list of images will be the lowest
common multiple of the different frame lengths.

If pal_ani=True, then also includes palette animations.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

TODO: The speed can be increased if we only re-render the changed animated tiles instead!

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.from_pil
documentation:
Import an entire map from one or two images (for each layer).
Changes all tiles, tilemappings and chunks in the BPC and re-writes the two layer mappings of the BMA.
Imports the palettes of the image to the BPL.
The palettes of the images passed into this method must either identical or can be merged.
The how_many_palettes_lower_layer parameter controls how many palettes
from the lower layer image will then be used.

The passed PIL will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index in the tile mapping for this
coordinate is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing up to 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

Does not import animations. BPA tiles must be manually mapped to the tilemappings of the BPC after the import.
BPL palette animations are not modified.

The input images must have the same dimensions as the BMA (same dimensions as to_pil_single_layer would export).
The input image can have a different number of layers, than the BMA. BPC and BMA layers are changed accordingly.

BMA collision and data layer are not modified.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.remove_upper_layer
documentation: Remove the upper layer. Silently does nothing when it doesn't exist.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.add_upper_layer
documentation: Add an upper layer. Silently does nothing when it already exists.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.resize
documentation:
Change the dimensions of the map. Existing tiles and chunks will keep their position in the grid.
If the size is reduced, all tiles and chunks that are moved out of the new dimension box are removed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.place_chunk
documentation: Place the chunk with the given ID at the X and Y position. No error checking is done.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.place_collision
documentation: Set the collision at the X and Y position. No error checking is done.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bma._model.Bma.place_data
documentation: Set data at the X and Y position. No error checking is done.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpci._model.Dpci.tiles_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all individual tiles of the DPCI into one PIL image.
The image contains all tiles next to each other, the image width is tile_width tiles.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

palettes is a list of 16 16 color palettes.
The tiles are exported with the first palette in the list of palettes.
The result image contains a palette that consists of all palettes merged together.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpci._model.Dpci.pil_to_tiles
documentation: Imports tiles that are in a format as described in the documentation for tiles_to_pil.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpci._model.Dpci.import_tiles
documentation:
Replace the tiles.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null tile is added to the list, at the beginning.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl.protocol.BplProtocol.import_palettes
documentation:
Replace all palettes with the ones passed in
Animated palette is not changed, but the number of spec entries is adjusted.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl.protocol.BplProtocol.apply_palette_animations
documentation:
Returns a modified copy of self.palettes.

This copy is modified to have colors swapped out for the current frame of palette animation.
The information for this is stored in self.animation_specs and the animation palette in
self.animation_palette.

Only available if self.has_palette_animation.

The maximum number of frames is the length of self.animation_palette

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl.protocol.BplProtocol.is_palette_affected_by_animation
documentation: Returns whether or not the palette with that index is affected by animation.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl.protocol.BplProtocol.get_real_palettes
documentation: Gets the actual palettes defined (without dummy grayscale entries).

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl.protocol.BplProtocol.set_palettes
documentation: Sets the palette properly, adding dummy grayscale entries if needed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl._model.Bpl.import_palettes
documentation:
Replace all palettes with the ones passed in
Animated palette is not changed, but the number of spec entries is adjusted.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl._model.Bpl.apply_palette_animations
documentation:
Returns a modified copy of self.palettes.

This copy is modified to have colors swapped out for the current frame of palette animation.
The information for this is stored in self.animation_specs and the animation palette in
self.animation_palette.

Only available if self.has_palette_animation.

The maximum number of frames is the length of self.animation_palette

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl._model.Bpl.is_palette_affected_by_animation
documentation: Returns whether or not the palette with that index is affected by animation.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl._model.Bpl.get_real_palettes
documentation: Gets the actual palettes defined (without dummy grayscale entries).

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpl._model.Bpl.set_palettes
documentation: Sets the palette properly, adding dummy grayscale entries if needed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpc.protocol.DpcProtocol.chunks_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all chunks of the DPC to one big PIL image.
The chunks are all placed next to each other.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

To be used with the DPCI file for this dungeon.
To get the palettes, use the data from the DPL file for this dungeon:

```pycon
>>> dpc.chunks_to_pil(dpci, dpl.palettes)
```

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpc.protocol.DpcProtocol.single_chunk_to_pil
documentation: Convert a single chunk of the DPC into a PIL image. For general notes, see chunks_to_pil.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpc.protocol.DpcProtocol.pil_to_chunks
documentation:
Imports chunks. Format same as for chunks_to_pil.
Replaces tile mappings and returns the new tiles for storing them in a DPCI and the palettes
for storing in a DPL.

The PIL must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpc.protocol.DpcProtocol.import_tile_mappings
documentation:
Replace the tile mappings of the specified layer.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null chunk is added to the list, at the beginning.

If correct_tile_ids is True, then the tile id of tile_mappings is also increased by one. Use this,
if you previously used import_tiles with contains_null_tile=False

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpla.protocol.DplaProtocol.get_palette_for_frame
documentation:
Returns the color palette at the given frame id. Returned is a stream of RGB colors: [R, G, B, R, G, B...].
Returned are always 16 colors. If the palette file has more than 16 colors, the pal_idx specifies what set
of 16 colors to return.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpla.protocol.DplaProtocol.get_duration_for_palette
documentation:
Warning: Colors in-game are animated separately. There is no speed for an entire palette.
We are asuming there's one speed for the entire palette.
This could be inaccurate.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpla.protocol.DplaProtocol.set_duration_for_palette
documentation:
Warning: Colors in-game are animated separately. There is no speed for an entire palette.
We are asuming there's one speed for the entire palette.
This could be inaccurate.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpla.protocol.DplaProtocol.apply_palette_animations
documentation:
Returns a modified copy of `palettes`.

This copy is modified to have colors swapped out for the current frame of palette animation.
> The first 16 colors of the DPLA model are placed in the palette 11 (if color 0 has at least one frame).
> The second 16 colors of the DPLA model are placed in the palette 12 (if color 16 has at least one frame).
> If the model has more colors, they are ignored.

Warning: Colors in-game are animated separately. There is no speed for an entire palette.
We are asuming there's one speed for the entire palette.
This could be inaccurate.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.handler.BpcHandler.deserialize
documentation:
Creates a BPC. A BPC contains two layers of image data. The image data is
grouped in 8x8 tiles, and these tiles are grouped in {tiling_width}x{tiling_height}
chunks using a tile mapping.
These chunks are referenced in the BMA tile to build the actual image.
The tiling sizes are also stored in the BMA file.
Each tile mapping is aso assigned a palette number. The palettes are stored in the BPL
file for the map background and always contain 16 colors.

The default for tiling_width and height are 3x3, because the game seems to be hardcoded this way.

documentation:

documentation:
Warning: Colors in-game are animated separately. There is no speed for an entire palette.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dpla._model.Dpla.apply_palette_animations
documentation:
Returns a modified copy of `palettes`.

This copy is modified to have colors swapped out for the current frame of palette animation.
> The first 16 colors of the DPLA model are placed in the palette 11 (if color 0 has at least one frame).
> The second 16 colors of the DPLA model are placed in the palette 12 (if color 16 has at least one frame).
> If the model has more colors, they are ignored.

Warning: Colors in-game are animated separately. There is no speed for an entire palette.
We are asuming there's one speed for the entire palette.
This could be inaccurate.

This palette file contains sets of 16 colors and "frames" of animations for them. Each Sir0 poiinter
points to one color entry and each of these color entries has 0-X frames of animation.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.__init__
documentation:
Creates a BPC. A BPC contains two layers of image data. The image data is
grouped in 8x8 tiles, and these tiles are grouped in {tiling_width}x{tiling_height}
chunks using a tile mapping.
These chunks are referenced in the BMA tile to build the actual image.
The tiling sizes are also stored in the BMA file.
Each tile mapping is aso asigned a palette number. The palettes are stored in the BPL
file for the map background and always contain 16 colors.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.chunks_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all chunks of the BPC to one big PIL image.
The chunks are all placed next to each other.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

To get the palettes, use the data from the BPL file for this map background:

```pycon
>>> bpc.chunks_to_pil(bpl.palettes)
```

The first chunk is a NULL tile. It is always empty, even when re-imported.

Does NOT export BPA tiles. Chunks that reference BPA tiles are replaced with empty tiles. You will see
warnings by the tiled_image module when this is the case.
The mapping to BPA tiles has to be done programmatically using set_tile or set_chunk.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.single_chunk_to_pil
documentation:
Convert a single chunk of the BPC to one big PIL image. For general notes, see chunks_to_pil.

Does NOT export BPA tiles. Chunks that reference BPA tiles are replaced with empty tiles.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.tiles_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all individual tiles of the BPC into one PIL image.
The image contains all tiles next to each other, the image width is tile_width tiles.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

The tiles are exported with the palette of the first placed tile or 0 if tile is not in tilemap,
for easier editing. The result image contains a palette that consists of all palettes merged together.

If single_palette is not None, all palettes are exported using the palette no. stored in single_palette.

The first tile is a NULL tile. It is always empty, even when re-imported.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.chunks_animated_to_pil
documentation:
Exports chunks. For general notes see chunks_to_pil.

However this method also exports BPA animated tiles referenced in the tilemap. This means it returns
a set of images containing the chunks, including BPC tiles and BPA tiles. BPA tiles are animated, and
each image contains one frame of the animation.

The method does not care about frame speeds. Each step of animation is simply returned as a new image,
so if BPAs use different frame speeds, this is ignored; they effectively run at the same speed.
If BPAs are using a different amount of frames per tile, the length of returned list of images will be the lowest
common denominator of the different frame lengths.

Does not include palette animations. You can apply them by switching out the palettes of the PIL
using the information provided by the BPL.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.single_chunk_animated_to_pil
documentation:
Exports a single chunk. For general notes see chunks_to_pil. For notes regarding the animation see
chunks_animated_to_pil.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.pil_to_tiles
documentation:
Imports tiles that are in a format as described in the documentation for tiles_to_pil.
Tile mappings, chunks and palettes are not updated.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.pil_to_chunks
documentation:
Imports chunks. Format same as for chunks_to_pil.
Replaces tiles, tile mappings and therefor also chunks.
"Unsets" BPA assignments! BPAs have to be manually re-assigned by using set_tile or set_chunk. BPA
indices are stored after BPC tile indices.

The PIL must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

Returns the palettes stored in the image for further processing (eg. replacing the BPL palettes).

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.import_tiles
documentation:
Replace the tiles of the specified layer.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null tile is added to the list, at the beginning.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.import_tile_mappings
documentation:
Replace the tile mappings of the specified layer.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null chunk is added to the list, at the beginning.

If correct_tile_ids is True, then the tile id of tile_mappings is also increased by one. Use this,
if you previously used import_tiles with contains_null_tile=False

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.get_bpas_for_layer
documentation:
This method returns a list of not None BPAs assigned to the BPC layer from an ordered list of possible candidates.
What is returned depends on the BPA mapping of the layer.

The bg_list.dat contains a list of 8 BPAs. The first four are for layer 0, the next four for layer 1.

This method asserts, that the number of tiles stored in the layer for the BPA, matches the data in the BPA!

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.remove_upper_layer
documentation: Remove the upper layer. Silently does nothing when it doesn't exist.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.add_upper_layer
documentation: Add an upper layer. Silently does nothing when it already exists.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc.protocol.BpcProtocol.process_bpa_change
documentation:
Update the layer entries for BPA tile number change and also re-map all tilemappings,
so that they still match their original tile, even though some tiles in-between may now
be new or removed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.__init__
documentation:
Creates a BPC. A BPC contains two layers of image data. The image data is
grouped in 8x8 tiles, and these tiles are grouped in {tiling_width}x{tiling_height}
chunks using a tile mapping.
These chunks are referenced in the BMA tile to build the actual image.
The tiling sizes are also stored in the BMA file.
Each tile mapping is aso asigned a palette number. The palettes are stored in the BPL
file for the map background and always contain 16 colors.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc._read_tile_data
documentation: Handles the decompressed tile data returned by the BPC_IMAGE decompressor.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc._read_tilemap_data
documentation: Handles the decompressed tile data returned by the BPC_TILEMAP decompressor.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.chunks_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all chunks of the BPC to one big PIL image.
The chunks are all placed next to each other.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

To get the palettes, use the data from the BPL file for this map background:

```pycon
>>> bpc.chunks_to_pil(bpl.palettes)
```

The first chunk is a NULL tile. It is always empty, even when re-imported.

Does NOT export BPA tiles. Chunks that reference BPA tiles are replaced with empty tiles. You will see
warnings by the tiled_image module when this is the case.
The mapping to BPA tiles has to be done programmatically using set_tile or set_chunk.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.single_chunk_to_pil
documentation:
Convert a single chunk of the BPC to one big PIL image. For general notes, see chunks_to_pil.

Does NOT export BPA tiles. Chunks that reference BPA tiles are replaced with empty tiles.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.tiles_to_pil
documentation:
Convert all individual tiles of the BPC into one PIL image.
The image contains all tiles next to each other, the image width is tile_width tiles.
The resulting image has one large palette with all palettes merged together.

The tiles are exported with the palette of the first placed tile or 0 if tile is not in tilemap,
for easier editing. The result image contains a palette that consists of all palettes merged together.

If single_palette is not None, all palettes are exported using the palette no. stored in single_palette.

The first tile is a NULL tile. It is always empty, even when re-imported.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.chunks_animated_to_pil
documentation:
Exports chunks. For general notes see chunks_to_pil.

However this method also exports BPA animated tiles referenced in the tilemap. This means it returns
a set of images containing the chunks, including BPC tiles and BPA tiles. BPA tiles are animated, and
each image contains one frame of the animation.

The method does not care about frame speeds. Each step of animation is simply returned as a new image,
so if BPAs use different frame speeds, this is ignored; they effectively run at the same speed.
If BPAs are using a different amount of frames per tile, the length of returned list of images will be the lowest
common denominator of the different frame lengths.

Does not include palette animations. You can apply them by switching out the palettes of the PIL
using the information provided by the BPL.

The list of bpas must be the one contained in the bg_list. It needs to contain 8 slots, with empty
slots being None.

TODO: The speed can be increased if we only re-render the changed animated tiles instead!

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.single_chunk_animated_to_pil
documentation:
Exports a single chunk. For general notes see chunks_to_pil. For notes regarding the animation see
chunks_animated_to_pil.

TODO: Code duplication with chunks_animated_to_pil. Could probably be refactored.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.pil_to_tiles
documentation:
Imports tiles that are in a format as described in the documentation for tiles_to_pil.
Tile mappings, chunks and palettes are not updated.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.pil_to_chunks
documentation:
Imports chunks. Format same as for chunks_to_pil.
Replaces tiles, tile mappings and therefor also chunks.
"Unsets" BPA assignments! BPAs have to be manually re-assigned by using set_tile or set_chunk. BPA
indices are stored after BPC tile indices.

The PIL must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

Returns the palettes stored in the image for further processing (eg. replacing the BPL palettes).

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.import_tiles
documentation:
Replace the tiles of the specified layer.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null tile is added to the list, at the beginning.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.import_tile_mappings
documentation:
Replace the tile mappings of the specified layer.
If contains_null_tile is False, the null chunk is added to the list, at the beginning.

If correct_tile_ids is True, then the tile id of tile_mappings is also increased by one. Use this,
if you previously used import_tiles with contains_null_tile=False

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.get_bpas_for_layer
documentation:
This method returns a list of not None BPAs assigned to the BPC layer from an ordered list of possible candidates.
What is returned depends on the BPA mapping of the layer.

The bg_list.dat contains a list of 8 BPAs. The first four are for layer 0, the next four for layer 1.

This method asserts, that the number of tiles stored in the layer for the BPA, matches the data in the BPA!

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.remove_upper_layer
documentation: Remove the upper layer. Silently does nothing when it doesn't exist.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.add_upper_layer
documentation: Add an upper layer. Silently does nothing when it already exists.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc.process_bpa_change
documentation:
Update the layer entries for BPA tile number change and also re-map all tilemappings,
so that they still match their original tile, even though some tiles in-between may now
be new or removed.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bpc._model.Bpc._get_palette_for_tile
documentation: Returns the first found palette of the tile with idx i. Or 0

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.kao._writer.KaoWriter.write
documentation:
Builds a new kao file as a BitStream.
The entire Kao starting from the first modified image needs
to be rebuilt, because the game expects the images in order and seems to use
the TOC pointers to indicate the size of image chunks, so there can be
no empty spaces.

This also updates the data representation of the original kao, unless
update_kao is False.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bgp.protocol.BgpProtocol.to_pil
documentation:
Convert all tiles of the BGP to one big PIL image.
The resulting image has one large palette with 256 colors.
If ignore_flip_bits is set, tiles are not flipped.

The image returned will have the size 256x192.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bgp.protocol.BgpProtocol.from_pil
documentation:
Modify the image data in the BGP by importing the passed PIL.
The passed PIL will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index
is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

The image must have the size 256x192.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bgp._model.Bgp.to_pil
documentation:
Convert all tiles of the BGP to one big PIL image.
The resulting image has one large palette with 256 colors.
If ignore_flip_bits is set, tiles are not flipped.

The image returned will have the size 256x192.
For dimension calculating, see the constants of this module.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bgp._model.Bgp.to_pil_tiled
documentation:
Convert all tiles of the BGP into separate PIL images.
Each image has one palette with 16 colors.
If ignore_flip_bits is set, tiles are not flipped.

768 tiles are returned, for dimension calculating, see the constants of this module.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.bgp._model.Bgp.from_pil
documentation:
Modify the image data in the BGP by importing the passed PIL.
The passed PIL will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index
is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing the 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

The image must have the size 256x192. For dimension calculating, see the constants of this module.

documentation: Place the chunk with the given ID at the X and Y position. No error checking is done.

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.dbg.protocol.DbgProtocol.from_pil
documentation:
Import an entire background from an image.
Changes all tiles, tile mappings and chunks in the DPC/DPCI and re-writes the mappings of the DBG.
Imports the palettes of the image to the DPL.

The passed PIL will be split into separate tiles and the tile's palette index in the tile mapping for this
coordinate is determined by the first pixel value of each tile in the PIL. The PIL
must have a palette containing up to 16 sub-palettes with 16 colors each (256 colors).

If a pixel in a tile uses a color outside of it's 16 color range, an error is thrown or
the color is replaced with 0 of the palette (transparent). This is controlled by
the force_import flag.

The input images must have the same dimensions as the DBG (same dimensions as to_pil_single_layer would export).

methods.name: skytemple_files.graphics.chara_wan.model.WanFile.ImportWan
documentation: This will accurately load all sir0 found in m_ground, m_attack, and monster.bin with a few exceptions:
m_ground_0546_0xb01840.wan - Armaldo.  Metaframe Unk#0 is a nonzero
m_ground_0587_0xbd0a10.wan - Latios.  Unknown difference
monster_0433_0x3dd1c0.sir0 - Giratina Origin.  Extra zeroes after file end.
monster_0438_0x3ec6d0.sir0 - Shaymin Sky.  Extra zeroes after file end.

classes.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.Operation
documentation:
All the possible operations that can be done to compress data!
Entries 0 to 8 correspond to their respective ctrl flag indexes!

classes.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.CompOp
documentation: Stores an operation to insert into the output buffer.

classes.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor.compress
documentation: Compresses the input data

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._handle_a_block
documentation:
Handle a block of 8 bytes and up to be compressed.
Return false, when has reached the end.

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._determine_best_operation
documentation: Determine and run the best possible operation to compress the data at cursor.

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._can_compress_to_2_in_1_byte
documentation: Check whether the 2 bytes at l_cusor can be stored as a single byte.

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._can_compress_to_2_in_1_byte_with_manipulation
documentation:
Check whether the 2 bytes at l_cursor can be stored as a single byte,
only if we use special operations based on the ctrl flag index contained
in the high nibble!

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._can_use_a_matching_sequence
documentation:
Search through the lookback buffer for a string of bytes that matches the
string beginning at l_cursor. It searches for at least 3 matching bytes
at first, then, finds the longest matching sequence it can!

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._find_longest_matching_sequence
documentation:
Find the longest matching sequence of at least PX_MIN_MATCH_SEQLEN bytes
and at most PX_MAX_MATCH_SEQLEN bytes.
- searchbeg      : Beginning of the zone to look for the sequence.
- searchend      : End of the zone to look for the sequence.
- tofindbeg      : Beginning of the sequence to find.
- tofindend      : End of the sequence to find.
- sequencelenght : Length of the sequence to look for in bytes.

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._check_sequence_high_nibble_valid_or_add
documentation:
Because the length is stored as the high nybble in the compressed output, and
that the high nybble also contains the ctrl flags, we need to make sure the
lengths of sequences to use do not overlap over values of the control flags !
So we'll build a list of length to reserve as we go!
-> If the value is in our reserved list, and we have PX_NB_POSSIBLE_SEQ_LEN
    of them already, return true.
-> If the value isn't in our reserved list, and we still have space left,
    add it and return true!
-> If the value isn't in our reserved list, and all PX_NB_POSSIBLE_SEQ_LEN
    slots are taken, return false!

NOTE:
    DO NOT pass the exact sequence length. The value stored in the
    high nybble is essentially : SequenceLen - PX_MIN_MATCH_SEQLEN

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._output_an_operation
documentation:
Outputs into the output buffer at position self.output_cursor the compressed
form of the operation passed in parameter!

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._build_ctrl_flags_list
documentation:
This determines all the control flags values, based on what matching
sequence lengths have been reserved so far !

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._output_all_operations
documentation:
This does the neccessary to execute all operations we put in our operation
queue. It also calculate the proper high nybble value for operation
using a control flag index !

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._adv_as_much_as_possible
documentation:
Advance an counter until either the given number of increments are made,
or the end is reached!

methods.name: skytemple_files.compression.px.compressor.PxCompressor._count_equal_consecutive_elem
documentation:
Count the amount of similar consecutive values between two sequences.
It stops counting once it stumbles on a differing value.
:return:
noinspection PyAttributeOutsideInit

This compression format is a modified version of
the AT6P container compression format used in 999
Changes made to the original algorithm are documented in the code
As this is based on AT6P,
the algorithm itself is based on this code (an AT6P decompressor):
https://github.com/pleonex/tinke/blob/master/Plugins/999HRPERDOOR/999HRPERDOOR/AT6P.cs

documentation:
Serialize the bin pack.

If fixed_header_len is set, the first sub file is placed at exactly this position,
the header is padded until then. For the three files in the MONSTER/ directory,
this must be 0x1300.

classes.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.binary_data_getter.BinaryDataGetter

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.binary_data_getter.BinaryDataGetter.get_binary_names
documentation:
Returns the names of all binaries that start with one of the specified strings, or all binaries if
starting_with is not specified.
\:param starting_with: If present, only binaries that start with one of the strings listed here will be returned.
\:return: List containing the names of all the binaries that matched the criteria

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.binary_data_getter.BinaryDataGetter.get_binary_section_protocol
documentation:
Given the name of a binary, returns its corresponding SectionProtocol instance on the current ROM.
\:param binary: Name of the binary to get
\:return: The SectionProtocol that corresponds to the given binary
\:raises ValueError: If a binary with the given name does not exist

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.binary_data_getter.BinaryDataGetter.has_data_symbols
documentation:
Checks if a given binary has at least one data symbol.
This method ignores deprecated symbols.
\:param binary: Name of the binary to check
\:return: True if the binary has at least one data symbol, false otherwise
\:raises ValueError: If a binary with the given name does not exist

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.binary_data_getter.BinaryDataGetter.get_data_symbols
documentation:
Given the name of a binary, returns the list of data symbols it contains.
This method ignores deprecated symbols.
\:param binary: Name of the binary to retrieve symbols from
\:return: List of data symbols for the given binary
\:raises ValueError: If a binary with the given name does not exist
Class used to retrieve information about binary sections defined for the current ROM and their symbols.

functions.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.manual.equivalent_types.get_size_equivalent_type
documentation:
In terms of size, some custom pmdsky-debug types are equivalent to simpler types. For example,
"struct monster_id_16" is equivalent to "uint16", and "enum monster_id" is equivalent to "uint32". This methods
attempts to get the size equivalent of a known complex type. If successful, returns the size equivalent type.
Otherwise, returns the original type.
\:param type_str: Original type
\:return If the original type has a simpler size equivalent type, returns that new type. Otherwise, returns the
original type.

functions.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.manual.equivalent_types.get_enum_equivalent_type
documentation:
Given a string that represents a C type, tries to convert it to its equivalent enum type representation.
For example, "struct monster_id_16" is equivalent to "enum monster_id".
If successful, returns the equivalent enum type. If not, returns the original type.
\:param type_str: Original type
\:return If the original type has a simpler enum equivalent type, returns that new type. Otherwise, returns the
original type.

in pmdsky-debug would require a lot of effort, we only document basic types here. This allows editing data for
symbols that use those types.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.c_type.CType.dim_down_array_type
documentation:
Creates a new instance of the class by taking an existing type that represents an array type and removing the

\:return: New instance of the class

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.c_type.CType.get_total_num_elements
documentation:
Returns the total number of elements this type contains across all its dimensions.
If it's not an array, returns 1.
\:return: Total number of elements on this type

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.c_type.CType.get_base_type_size
documentation:
Gets the size of the base type in bytes. Not all types support this operation.
\:return: Size of the base type represented by this instance, in bytes
\:raises UnsupportedTypeError: If this type does not support this operation.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.c_type.CType.get_size
documentation:
Gets the total size of this type in bytes. Not all types support this operation.
\:return: Total size of this type, in bytes
\:raises UnsupportedTypeError: If this type does not support this operation.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.c_type.CType.get_multi_dimension_index
documentation:
Given a linear index between 0 and the product of all elements in self.array_size (upper bound exclusive),
returns the equivalent index for each dimension.
For example, if array_size is [10][3], and linear_index is 5, the result would be [1, 2].
\:param linear_index: Linear index to convert
\:return: Indexes on each of the dimensions of this type that correspond to the provided linear index
\:raises ValueError If this type is not an array type
Represents a C type, and its array dimensions if it's an array type.

classes.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.rw_symbol.RWSymbol

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.rw_symbol.RWSymbol.from_basic_data
documentation:
Attempts to create a new instance of this class given basic symbol information. The exact subclass of the
instance will depend on the specified type string.
\:param name: Name of the symbol
\:param offset: Offset within the binary where data will be read and written to
\:param type_str: Type of the symbol
\:return: New RWSymbol instance
\:raises UnsupportedTypeError: If the type of the input symbol is not supported by the RWSymbol subclasses.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.rw_symbol.RWSymbol.from_symbol
documentation:
Attempts to create a new RWSymbol with the information from the given symbol.
\:param symbol: Symbol to use to create the RWSymbol. Must have exactly one address and a type.
\:return: New RWSymbol instance
\:raises UnsupportedTypeError: If the type of the input symbol is not supported by the RWSymbol subclasses.
\:raises ValueError: If the provided symbol does not have exactly one address or it does not have a type.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.rw_symbol.RWSymbol.read_str_with_path
documentation:
Allows reading the value of this symbol. If it's a compound symbol (like an array or a struct), this method
allows reading the value of any sub-symbols contained in this symbol.
\:param binary: Binary to read the data from
\:param path: Path to the symbol to read
\:return: The value of the specified symbol or sub-symbol, as a string
\:raises ValueError: If the specified path is invalid

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.rw_symbol.RWSymbol.write_str_with_path
documentation:
Allows writing a value to this symbol. If it's a compound symbol (like an array or a struct), this method
allows writing a value to any sub-symbols contained in this symbol.
\:param binary: Binary to write the data to
\:param path: Path to the symbol to read
\:param value: Value to write, as a string. It will be converted to the appropriate type beforehand.
\:raises ValueError: If the specified path is invalid

The RWSymbol class, short for "Readable/Writable Symbol", can be used to read/write the value of a pmdsky-debug
symbol to a given binary.

classes.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.symbol_path.SymbolPath

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.symbol_path.SymbolPath.get_next_array
documentation:
Returns the next section of the path, assuming it's an array section. Also returns the rest of the path
after removing the first section.
\:return: Tuple of two elements. The first element will contain the next section of the path, as a list of
integers, each representing an array index. The second will contain the rest of the path after removing the
first element.
\:raises ValueError: If the next section of the path is not an array section.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.symbol_path.SymbolPath.get_next_array_flat
documentation:
Same as get_next_array, but all dimensions of the array section will be merged together. Their total size
will be returned as a single integer.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.symbols.symbol_path.SymbolPath.get_next_field
documentation:
Returns the next section of the path, assuming it's a struct field. Also returns the rest of the path
after removing the first section.
\:return: Tuple of two elements. The first element will contain the next section of the path, as a string
representing the name of the struct field. The second will contain the rest of the path after removing the
first element.
\:raises ValueError: If the next section of the path is not a struct field.

String that represents a path though the values of multiple compound symbols.
It uses C-like notation to represent array values and struct fields.
Example: Following path "[12].some_field[3]" would access element #12 of this symbol, which should be a struct.
Then it would access struct field "some_field", which should be an array, and would finally access element #3 on
that array.

modules.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.monster_sprite_data_table
documentation:
This table is stored in ARM9 and has two entries for every Pokémon base form.
- The first one seems to be how many 16x16 tile slots (or 256 byte pixels) the
  Pokémon's sprite will take up.
- The second is unknown, but also related to the sprite size?

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.ExtraDungeonDataEntry._valid_index
documentation:
Returns true if a given guest pokémon index is valid.
Valid values are indexes 0 to 62 and -1, which is used as an empty value

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.ExtraDungeonDataList.read
documentation: Returns the list of extra dungeon data

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.ExtraDungeonDataList.write
documentation:
Writes the list of dungeon extra data to the arm9 binary provided.
The list must have exactly 0xB4 entries.

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.GuestPokemonList.get_max_entries
documentation: Returns the maximum amount of entries that can fit in the binary

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.GuestPokemonList.read
documentation: Returns the list of guest pokémon data

methods.name: skytemple_files.hardcoded.guest_pokemon.GuestPokemonList.write
documentation:
Writes the list of guest pokémon data to the arm9 binary.
The first 18 entries will be written to the GuestPokemonData block, the rest
will be written to GuestPokemonData2.
The list can't have more entries than the max amount specified by get_max_entries()

classes.name: skytemple_files.dungeon_data.mappa_g_bin.model.StubMappaGFloor
documentation: A mappa_g floor that only references an index for all of it's data.

methods.name: skytemple_files.dungeon_data.mappa_g_bin.model.MappaGBin.minimize
documentation:
Collects a list of floors, that references indices in other lists, like stored in the mappa_g files.
If two floors use the same exact data for something, they will be pointing to the same index in the lists,
there are no duplicates.
Returned are all lists.

methods.name: skytemple_files.dungeon_data.mappa_bin._python_impl.model.MappaBin.minimize
documentation:
Collects a list of floors, that references indices in other lists, like stored in the mappa files.
If two floors use the same exact data for something, they will be pointing to the same index in the lists,
there are no duplicates.
Returned are all lists.
TODO: Performance could be improved here, by using more efficient lookup mechanisms.

classes.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.name
documentation: The name for this patch. It must be the same as in the ppmdu configuration file.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.description
documentation: A description text for your patch.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.author
documentation: The author of the patch.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.version
documentation:
The version for this patch as a string. Only used for displaying it to the user,
but may be used by the handler to update the patch.
Must follow Python package version conventions.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.category
documentation: The category describing the purpose of this patch.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.is_applied
documentation:
Returns True, if the patch is applied, False otherwise.
Raises NotImplementedError if ROM not supported.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.apply
documentation:
Apply the patch. The apply parameter is a callable which applies the actual patch files using ARMIPS,
as specified in the configuration file.
The `apply` callable might raise a RuntimeError, if the patch was not applied successfully.
It raises a ArmipsNotInstalledError when armips is not installed.
The configuration (`config`) may be invalid (no longer up to date) after calling `apply`.

```
param apply

Applies the ROM patch

param rom

Nintendo DS ROM that is being patched.

param config

ppmdu configuration for the ROM to be patched.

raises RuntimeError

On error applying the patch. If this is raised, the ROM will not be modified.

raises ValueError

On error applying the patch. If this is raised, the ROM will not be modified.
```

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.unapply
documentation:
TODO: Not supported yet. For future implementation.
See apply method for info on how it will work.
May raise NotImplementedError, if not supported.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.get_parameter
documentation:
Returns the given configuration parameter. Make sure it is defined.
\:param name: name of the parameter

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.get_parameters
documentation: Returns all given configuration parameters or an empty dict of nothing was given.

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.AbstractPatchHandler.supply_parameters
documentation: Only to be called by the patch handler: Sets the configuration parameters.
noinspection PyAttributeOutsideInit
A handler for applying a ROM patch to PMD EoS.

classes.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.DependantPatch

methods.name: skytemple_files.patch.handler.abstract.DependantPatch.depends_on
documentation: A list of patches (names) that need to be applied before this patch can be applied.
Extra interface to be implemented by patches that require other patches to be applied first.
